''Forked from the [https://github.com/source-academy/js-slang/wiki/SVML-Instruction-Set SVML Instruction Set] in the js-slang wiki. See [[PVML-Specification]] for the relationship between SVML/Sinter and PVML/Pynter. The opcodes below (0x00-0x54) are identical to Sinter's today, since Pynter is currently an unmodified fork; the "Primitive functions" section, however, does '''not''' match py-slang's own primitive index table (`src/engines/svml/builtins.ts`) — see the note there.''

== PVML instruction set ==

__TOC__

=== Faults ===

Faults specific to particular instructions are documented in the entries below. In general, such faults are due to:

* type errors, for instructions that operate on boxed values
* invalid array indexes (negative or non-integral)
* incorrect function arity

<p>Aside from those, the following faults can be raised by any instruction; however, these faults are indicative of bugs in the compiler. A correct compiler should not produce programs that result in the following faults.</p>

* Pushing onto a full stack
* Popping from an empty stack
* Loading from or storing into an invalid index in an environment
* Running out of memory

=== Mnemonic conventions ===

For instructions that operate on values, the type of the operand(s) is represented in the instruction mnemonic by a letter:

* <code>u</code> undefined
* <code>n</code> null
* <code>b</code> boolean
* <code>i</code> integer
* <code>f</code> number (float)
* <code>s</code> string
* <code>a</code> array
* <code>c</code> function
* <code>g</code> boxed value

=== Instruction entry format ===

==== <code>mnemonic</code>: instruction name ====

Format: <code>opcode <argument1: type> <argument2: type></code> (N bytes)

The binary format byte count is omitted for instructions with no arguments. They are 1 byte long.

Stack before: …, <code>value1: type</code>, <code>value2: type</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code>value3: type</code> ■

■ represents the top of the stack.

The rest of the entry details what the opcode does, and any possible faults.

=== Instructions ===

==== <code>nop</code>: no-op ====

Format: <code>0x00</code>

Does not modify the stack.

Does nothing.

==== <code>ldc.i</code>: load constant integer ====

Format: <code>0x01 <value: i32></code> (5 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><value>: number</code> ■

Pushes an integer with value equal to <code><value></code> onto the stack.

==== <code>lgc.i</code>: load boxed constant integer ====

Format: <code>0x02 <value: i32></code> (5 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><value>: boxed (number)</code> ■

Pushes a boxed integer with value equal to <code><value></code> onto the stack.

==== <code>ldc.f32</code>: load constant number (single-precision) ====

Format: <code>0x03 <number: f32></code> (5 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><number>: number</code> ■

Pushes a number with value equal to <code><number></code> onto the stack.

==== <code>lgc.f32</code>: load boxed constant number (single-precision) ====

Format: <code>0x04 <number: f32></code> (5 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><number>: boxed (number)</code> ■

Pushes a boxed number with value equal to <code><number></code> onto the stack.

==== <code>ldc.f64</code>: load constant number (double-precision) ====

Format: <code>0x05 <number: f64></code> (9 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><number>: number</code> ■

Pushes a number with value equal to <code><number></code> onto the stack.

==== <code>lgc.f64</code>: load boxed constant number (double-precision) ====

Format: <code>0x06 <number: f64></code> (9 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><number>: boxed (number)</code> ■

Pushes a boxed number with value equal to <code><number></code> onto the stack.

==== <code>ldc.b.0</code>: load constant false ====

Format: <code>0x07</code>

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code>false: boolean</code> ■

Pushes the boolean <code>false</code> onto the stack.

==== <code>ldc.b.1</code>: load constant true ====

Format: <code>0x08</code>

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code>true: boolean</code> ■

Pushes the boolean <code>true</code> onto the stack.

==== <code>lgc.b.0</code>: load boxed constant false ====

Format: <code>0x09</code>

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code>false: boxed (boolean)</code> ■

Pushes a boxed boolean <code>false</code> onto the stack.

==== <code>lgc.b.1</code>: load boxed constant true ====

Format: <code>0x0A</code>

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code>true: boxed (boolean)</code> ■

Pushes a boxed boolean <code>true</code> onto the stack.

==== <code>lgc.u</code>: load boxed constant undefined ====

Format: <code>0x0B</code>

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code>undefined: boxed (undefined)</code> ■

Pushes a boxed <code>undefined</code> onto the stack.

==== <code>lgc.n</code>: load boxed constant null ====

Format: <code>0x0C</code>

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code>null: boxed (null)</code> ■

Pushes a boxed <code>null</code> onto the stack.

==== <code>lgc.s</code>: load constant string ====

Format: <code>0x0D <address></code> (5 bytes)

In the JSON format, the string literal is specified directly instead.

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><string>: boxed (string)</code> ■

Pushes the string at the given address onto the stack.

The string at the given address should be null-terminated.

==== <code>pop.g</code>: pop boxed value from stack ====

Format: <code>0x0E</code>

Stack before: …, <code><value>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: … ■

Pops a boxed value off the stack.

Behaviour is undefined if <code><value></code> is not a boxed value.

==== <code>pop.b</code>: pop boolean from stack ====

Format: <code>0x0F</code>

Stack before: …, <code><value>: boolean</code> ■

Stack after: … ■

Pops a boolean off the stack.

Behaviour is undefined if <code><value></code> is not a boolean.

==== <code>pop.f</code>: pop number from stack ====

Format: <code>0x10</code>

Stack before: …, <code><value>: number</code> ■

Stack after: … ■

Pops a number off the stack.

Behaviour is undefined if <code><value></code> is not a number.

==== <code>add.g</code>: add boxed values ====

Format: <code>0x11</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code>, <code><b>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boxed</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not boxed values, behaviour is undefined.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are strings, pushes their concatenation <code><c> = <a> + <b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are numbers, pushes their sum <code><c> = <a> + <b></code> onto the stack.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

==== <code>add.f</code>: add numbers ====

Format: <code>0x12</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: number</code>, <code><b>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: number</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack, then pushes their sum <code><c> = <a> + <b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not numbers, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>sub.g</code>: subtract boxed values ====

Format: <code>0x13</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code>, <code><b>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boxed</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not boxed values, behaviour is undefined.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are numbers, pushes their difference <code><c> = <a> - <b></code> onto the stack.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

==== <code>sub.f</code>: subtract numbers ====

Format: <code>0x14</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: number</code>, <code><b>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: number</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack, then pushes their difference <code><c> = <a> - <b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not numbers, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>mul.g</code>: multiply boxed values ====

Format: <code>0x15</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code>, <code><b>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boxed</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not boxed values, behaviour is undefined.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are numbers, pushes their product <code><c> = <a> * <b></code> onto the stack.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

==== <code>mul.f</code>: multiply numbers ====

Format: <code>0x16</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: number</code>, <code><b>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: number</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack, then pushes their product <code><c> = <a> * <b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not numbers, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>div.g</code>: divide boxed values ====

Format: <code>0x17</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code>, <code><b>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boxed</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not boxed values, behaviour is undefined.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are numbers, pushes their quotient <code><c> = <a> / <b></code> onto the stack.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

==== <code>div.f</code>: divide numbers ====

Format: <code>0x18</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: number</code>, <code><b>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: number</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack, then pushes their quotient <code><c> = <a> / <b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not numbers, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>mod.g</code>: modulo boxed values ====

Format: <code>0x19</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code>, <code><b>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boxed</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not boxed values, behaviour is undefined.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are numbers, pushes <code><c> = <a> % <b></code> onto the stack.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

==== <code>mod.f</code>: modulo numbers ====

Format: <code>0x1A</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: number</code>, <code><b>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: number</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack, then pushes <code><c> = <a> % <b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not numbers, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>neg.g</code>: negate boxed value ====

Format: <code>0x50</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><b>: boxed</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> off the stack.

If <code><a></code> is not a boxed value, behaviour is undefined.

If <code><a></code> is a number, pushes <code><b> = -<a></code> onto the stack.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

==== <code>neg.f</code>: negate number ====

Format: <code>0x51</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><b>: number</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> off the stack, then pushes <code><b> = -<a></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> is not a number, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>not.g</code>: negate boxed value ====

Format: <code>0x1B</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><b>: boxed</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> off the stack.

If <code><a></code> is a boolean, pushes its negation <code><b></code> onto the stack.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

==== <code>not.b</code>: negate boolean ====

Format: <code>0x1C</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boolean</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><b>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> off the stack, then pushes its negation <code><b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> is not a boolean, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>lt.g</code>: less than, boxed operands ====

Format: <code>0x1D</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code>, <code><b>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not boxed values, behaviour is undefined.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are both strings or both numbers, pushes <code><c> = <a> < <b></code>, with the obvious meaning if both operands are numbers, and comparing the operands by lexicographical order if they are strings.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

==== <code>lt.f</code>: less than, number operands ====

Format: <code>0x1E</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: number</code>, <code><b>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack, then pushes <code><c> = <a> < <b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not numbers, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>gt.g</code>: greater than, boxed operands ====

Format: <code>0x1F</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code>, <code><b>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not boxed values, behaviour is undefined.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are both strings or both numbers, pushes <code><c> = <a> > <b></code>, with the obvious meaning if both operands are numbers, and comparing the operands by lexicographical order if they are strings.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

==== <code>gt.f</code>: greater than, number operands ====

Format: <code>0x20</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: number</code>, <code><b>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack, then pushes <code><c> = <a> > <b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not numbers, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>le.g</code>: less than or equal to, boxed operands ====

Format: <code>0x21</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code>, <code><b>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not boxed values, behaviour is undefined.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are both strings or both numbers, pushes <code><c> = <a> <= <b></code>, with the obvious meaning if both operands are numbers, and comparing the operands by lexicographical order if they are strings.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

==== <code>le.f</code>: less than or equal to, number operands ====

Format: <code>0x22</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: number</code>, <code><b>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack, then pushes <code><c> = <a> <= <b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not numbers, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>ge.g</code>: greater than or equal to, boxed operands ====

Format: <code>0x23</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code>, <code><b>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not boxed values, behaviour is undefined.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are both strings or both numbers, pushes <code><c> = <a> >= <b></code>, with the obvious meaning if both operands are numbers, and comparing the operands by lexicographical order if they are strings.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

==== <code>ge.f</code>: greater than or equal to, number operands ====

Format: <code>0x24</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: number</code>, <code><b>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack, then pushes <code><c> = <a> >= <b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not numbers, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>eq.g</code>: equal, boxed operands ====

Format: <code>0x25</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code>, <code><b>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not boxed values, behaviour is undefined.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are of different types, pushes <code>false</code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are both <code>undefined</code> or both <code>null</code>, pushes <code>true</code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are both booleans, both numbers or both strings, pushes <code>true</code> onto the stack if they have the same value, otherwise pushes <code>false</code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are both functions or both arrays, pushes <code>true</code> onto the stack if they are referentially equal i.e. they refer to the exact same array or function object, otherwise pushes <code>false</code> onto the stack.

The above cases are exhaustive.

==== <code>eq.f</code>: equal, number operands ====

Format: <code>0x26</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: number</code>, <code><b>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack, then pushes <code><c> = <a> == <b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not numbers, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>eq.b</code>: equal, boolean operands ====

Format: <code>0x27</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boolean</code>, <code><b>: boolean</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack, then pushes <code><c> = <a> == <b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not booleans, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>neq.g</code>: not equal, boxed operands ====

Format: <code>0x52</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code>, <code><b>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not boxed values, behaviour is undefined.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are of different types, pushes <code>true</code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are both <code>undefined</code> or both <code>null</code>, pushes <code>false</code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are both booleans, both numbers or both strings, pushes <code>false</code> onto the stack if they have the same value, otherwise pushes <code>true</code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are both functions or both arrays, pushes <code>false</code> onto the stack if they are referentially equal i.e. they refer to the exact same array or function object, otherwise pushes <code>true</code> onto the stack.

The above cases are exhaustive.

==== <code>neq.f</code>: not equal, number operands ====

Format: <code>0x53</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: number</code>, <code><b>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack, then pushes <code><c> = <a> != <b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not numbers, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>neq.b</code>: not equal, boolean operands ====

Format: <code>0x54</code>

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boolean</code>, <code><b>: boolean</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> off the stack, then pushes <code><c> = <a> != <b></code> onto the stack.

If <code><a></code> and <code><b></code> are not booleans, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>new.c</code>: create function ====

Format: <code>0x28 <address></code> (5 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boxed (function)</code> ■

Pushes a new function object <code><c></code> onto the stack referring to the function at the given address.

==== <code>new.c.p</code>: create function object for primitive function ====

Format: <code>0x4E <id: u8></code> (2 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boxed (function)</code> ■

Pushes a new function object <code><c></code> onto the stack that represents the primitive function with the given <code><id></code>.

==== <code>new.c.v</code>: create function object for VM-internal function ====

Format: <code>0x4F <id: u8></code> (2 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><c>: boxed (function)</code> ■

Pushes a new function object <code><c></code> onto the stack that represents the VM-internal function with the given <code><id></code>.

==== <code>new.a</code>: create array ====

Format: <code>0x29</code>

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><a>: boxed (array)</code> ■

Pushes a new empty array onto the stack.

==== <code>ldl.g</code>: load boxed value from current environment ====

Format: <code>0x2A <index: u8></code> (2 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><a>: boxed</code> ■

Pushes the value <code><a></code> at index <code>index</code> in the current environment onto the stack.

If the value at index <code>index</code> in the current environment is not a boxed value, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>ldl.f</code>: load number from current environment ====

Format: <code>0x2B <index: u8></code> (2 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><a>: number</code> ■

Pushes the value <code><a></code> at index <code>index</code> in the current environment onto the stack.

If the value at index <code>index</code> in the current environment is not a number, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>ldl.b</code>: load boolean from current environment ====

Format: <code>0x2C <index: u8></code> (2 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><a>: boolean</code> ■

Pushes the value <code><a></code> at index <code>index</code> in the current environment onto the stack.

If the value at index <code>index</code> in the current environment is not a boolean, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>stl.g</code>: store boxed value into current environment ====

Format: <code>0x2D <index: u8></code> (2 bytes)

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: … ■

Pops <code><a></code> off the stack and stores it in index <code>index</code> in the current environment.

If <code><a></code> is not a boxed value, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>stl.b</code>: store boolean into current environment ====

Format: <code>0x2E <index: u8></code> (2 bytes)

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boolean</code> ■

Stack after: … ■

Pops <code><a></code> off the stack and stores it in index <code>index</code> in the current environment.

If <code><a></code> is not a boolean, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>stl.f</code>: store number into current environment ====

Format: <code>0x2F <index: u8></code> (2 bytes)

Stack before: …, <code><a>: number</code> ■

Stack after: … ■

Pops <code><a></code> off the stack and stores it in index <code>index</code> in the current environment.

If <code><a></code> is not a number, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>ldp.g</code>: load boxed value from (parent) environment ====

Format: <code>0x30 <index: u8> <envindex: u8></code> (3 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><a>: boxed</code> ■

Pushes the value at index <code>index</code> in the <code>envindex</code>th parent of the current environment onto the stack. If <code>envindex</code> is <code>0</code>, this is equivalent to <code>ldl.g</code>.

If the value is not a boxed value, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>ldp.f</code>: load number from (parent) environment ====

Format: <code>0x31 <index: u8> <envindex: u8></code> (3 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><a>: number</code> ■

Pushes the value at index <code>index</code> in the <code>envindex</code>th parent of the current environment onto the stack. If <code>envindex</code> is <code>0</code>, this is equivalent to <code>ldl.f</code>.

If the value is not a number, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>ldp.b</code>: load boolean from (parent) environment ====

Format: <code>0x32 <index: u8> <envindex: u8></code> (3 bytes)

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: …, <code><a>: boolean</code> ■

Pushes the value at index <code>index</code> in the <code>envindex</code>th parent of the current environment onto the stack. If <code>envindex</code> is <code>0</code>, this is equivalent to <code>ldl.f</code>.

If the value is not a boolean, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>stp.g</code>: store boxed value into (parent) environment ====

Format: <code>0x33 <index: u8> <envindex: u8></code> (3 bytes)

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: … ■

Pops <code><a></code> off the stack, and stores <code><a></code> into index <code>index</code> in the <code>envindex</code>th parent of the current environment.

If <code><a></code> is not a boxed value, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>stp.b</code>: store boolean into (parent) environment ====

Format: <code>0x34 <index: u8> <envindex: u8></code> (3 bytes)

Stack before: …, <code><a>: boolean</code> ■

Stack after: … ■

Pops <code><a></code> off the stack, and stores <code><a></code> into index <code>index</code> in the <code>envindex</code>th parent of the current environment.

If <code><a></code> is not a boolean, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>stp.f</code>: store number into (parent) environment ====

Format: <code>0x35 <index: u8> <envindex: u8></code> (3 bytes)

Stack before: …, <code><a>: number</code> ■

Stack after: … ■

Pops <code><a></code> off the stack, and stores <code><a></code> into index <code>index</code> in the <code>envindex</code>th parent of the current environment.

If <code><a></code> is not a number, behaviour is undefined.

==== <code>lda.g</code>: load boxed value from array ====

Format: <code>0x36</code>

Stack before: …, <code><array>: boxed (array)</code>, <code><index>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><value>: boxed</code> ■

Pops <code>index</code> and <code>array</code> off the stack.

If <code>index</code> is a non-negative integer, pushes the value at index <code>index</code> in <code>array</code> onto the stack.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

Behaviour is undefined if the value at index <code>index</code> in array is not a boxed value.

==== <code>lda.b</code>: load boolean from array ====

Format: <code>0x37</code>

Stack before: …, <code><array>: boxed (array)</code>, <code><index>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><value>: boolean</code> ■

Pops <code>index</code> and <code>array</code> off the stack.

If <code>index</code> is a non-negative integer, pushes the value at index <code>index</code> in <code>array</code> onto the stack.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

Behaviour is undefined if the value at index <code>index</code> in array is not a boolean.

==== <code>lda.f</code>: load number from array ====

Format: <code>0x38</code>

Stack before: …, <code><array>: boxed (array)</code>, <code><index>: number</code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><value>: number</code> ■

Pops <code>index</code> and <code>array</code> off the stack.

If <code>index</code> is a non-negative integer, pushes the value at index <code>index</code> in <code>array</code> onto the stack.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

Behaviour is undefined if the value at index <code>index</code> in array is not a number.

==== <code>sta.g</code>: store boxed value into array ====

Format: <code>0x39</code>

Stack before: …, <code><array>: boxed (array)</code>, <code><index>: number</code>, <code><value>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: … ■

Pops <code>value</code>, <code>index</code> and <code>array</code> off the stack.

If <code>index</code> is a non-negative integer, stores <code>value</code> into index <code>index</code> in <code>array</code>.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

Behaviour is undefined if <code>value</code> is not a boxed value.

==== <code>sta.b</code>: store boolean into array ====

Format: <code>0x3A</code>

Stack before: …, <code><array>: boxed (array)</code>, <code><index>: number</code>, <code><value>: boolean</code> ■

Stack after: … ■

Pops <code>value</code>, <code>index</code> and <code>array</code> off the stack.

If <code>index</code> is a non-negative integer, stores <code>value</code> into index <code>index</code> in <code>array</code>.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

Behaviour is undefined if <code>value</code> is not a boolean.

==== <code>sta.f</code>: store number into array ====

Format: <code>0x3B</code>

Stack before: …, <code><array>: boxed (array)</code>, <code><index>: number</code>, <code><value>: number</code> ■

Stack after: … ■

Pops <code>value</code>, <code>index</code> and <code>array</code> off the stack.

If <code>index</code> is a non-negative integer, stores <code>value</code> into index <code>index</code> in <code>array</code>.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

Behaviour is undefined if <code>value</code> is not a number.

==== <code>br.t</code>: branch if true ====

Format: <code>0x3C <offset></code> (5 bytes)

Stack before: …, <code><condition>: boolean</code> ■

Stack after: … ■

Pops <code>condition</code> off the stack.

If <code>condition</code> is a boolean and is true, skips <code>offset</code> bytes starting from after the current instruction. That is, <code>br.t 0</code> is a no-op.

Note that <code>offset</code> can be negative.

If <code>condition</code> is a boolean and is false, does nothing.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

==== <code>br.f</code>: branch if false ====

Format: <code>0x3D <offset></code> (5 bytes)

Stack before: …, <code><condition>: boolean</code> ■

Stack after: … ■

Pops <code>condition</code> off the stack.

If <code>condition</code> is a boolean and is false, skips <code>offset</code> bytes starting from after the current instruction. That is, <code>br.f 0</code> is a no-op.

Note that <code>offset</code> can be negative.

If <code>condition</code> is a boolean and is true, does nothing.

Otherwise, a fault occurs.

==== <code>br</code>: branch ====

Format: <code>0x3E <offset></code> (5 bytes)

Does not modify the stack.

Skips <code>offset</code> bytes starting from after the current instruction. That is, <code>br 0</code> is a no-op.

Note that <code>offset</code> can be negative.

==== <code>jmp</code>: jump ====

Format: <code>0x3F <address></code> (5 bytes)

Jumps to <code>address</code>. Note: the current environment and stack are unchanged.

==== <code>call</code>: call function ====

Format: <code>0x40 <numargs: u8></code> (2 bytes)

Stack before: …, <code><f>: boxed (function)</code>, <code><a1></code>, <code><a2></code>, …, <code><aN></code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><r></code> ■

Pops the arguments to be passed to the function off the stack in reverse order, followed by the function to be called. That is, pop the last argument, followed by the second last, and so on, until the first argument, then the function.

Calls the function, and then pushes the return value of the function onto the stack.

The type of arguments to be popped, and the type of the return value, is determined by the function header as referred to by the function object.

Behaviour is undefined if arguments on the stack are of different types compared to the types specified in the function header.

A fault occurs if the number of arguments specified in <code><numargs></code> is different from the number of arguments expected by the function as specified in the function header.

==== <code>call.t</code>: tail call function ====

Format: <code>0x41 <numargs: u8></code> (2 bytes)

Stack before: …, <code><f>: boxed (function)</code>, <code><a1></code>, <code><a2></code>, …, <code><aN></code> ■

Stack after: (the stack is destroyed)

Pops the arguments to be passed to the function off the stack in reverse order, followed by the function to be called. That is, pop the last argument, followed by the second last, and so on, until the first argument, then the function.

Calls the function. The return value of the callee function will become the return value of the current function, and execution returns to the caller of the current function.

The type of arguments to be popped, and the type of the return value, is determined by the function header as referred to by the function object.

Behaviour is undefined if arguments on the stack are of different types compared to the types specified in the function header.

A fault occurs if the number of arguments specified in <code><numargs></code> is different from the number of arguments expected by the function as specified in the function header.

==== <code>call.p</code>: call primitive function ====

Format: <code>0x42 <id: u8> <numargs: u8></code> (3 bytes)

Stack before: …, <code><a1></code>, <code><a2></code>, …, <code><aN></code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><r></code> ■

Pops the arguments to be passed to the function off the stack in reverse order. That is, pop the last argument, followed by the second last, and so on, until the first argument.

Calls the primitive function with the given <code>id</code>, and then pushes the return value of the function onto the stack.

The types of the arguments are as specified in the list of primitive functions.

Behaviour is undefined if arguments on the stack are of different types compared to the specified types.

==== <code>call.t.p</code>: tail call primitive function ====

Format: <code>0x43 <id: u8> <numargs: u8></code> (3 bytes)

Stack before: …, <code><a1></code>, <code><a2></code>, …, <code><aN></code> ■

Stack after: (the stack is destroyed)

Pops the arguments to be passed to the function off the stack in reverse order. That is, pop the last argument, followed by the second last, and so on, until the first argument.

Calls the primitive function with the given <code>id</code>. The return value of the primitive function will become the return value of the current function, and execution returns to the caller of the current function.

The types of the arguments are as specified in the list of primitive functions.

Behaviour is undefined if arguments on the stack are of different types compared to the specified types.

==== <code>call.v</code>: call VM-internal function/native function ====

Format: <code>0x44 <id: u8> <numargs: u8></code> (3 bytes)

Stack before: …, <code><a1></code>, <code><a2></code>, …, <code><aN></code> ■

Stack after: …, <code><r></code> ■

Pops the arguments to be passed to the function off the stack in reverse order. That is, pop the last argument, followed by the second last, and so on, until the first argument.

Calls the VM-internal function with the given <code>id</code>, and then pushes the return value of the function onto the stack.

VM-internal functions are specified by the VM implementation.

Behaviour is undefined if arguments on the stack are of different types compared to the specified types.

==== <code>call.t.v</code>: tail call VM-internal function/native function ====

Format: <code>0x45 <id: u8> <numargs: u8></code> (3 bytes)

Stack before: …, <code><a1></code>, <code><a2></code>, …, <code><aN></code> ■

Stack after: (the stack is destroyed)

Pops the arguments to be passed to the function off the stack in reverse order. That is, pop the last argument, followed by the second last, and so on, until the first argument.

Calls the VM-internal function with the given <code>id</code>. The return value of the VM-internal function will become the return value of the current function, and execution returns to the caller of the current function.

VM-internal functions are specified by the VM implementation.

Behaviour is undefined if arguments on the stack are of different types compared to the specified types.

==== <code>ret.g</code>: return boxed value ====

Format: <code>0x46</code>

Stack before: …, <code><retval>: boxed</code> ■

Stack after: (the stack is destroyed)

Pops <code>retval</code> off the stack. Makes <code>retval</code> the return value of the current function, and returns execution to the caller of the current function.

Behaviour is undefined if <code>retval</code> is not a boxed value.

==== <code>ret.f</code>: return number ====

Format: <code>0x47</code>

Stack before: …, <code><retval>: number</code> ■

Stack after: (the stack is destroyed)

Pops <code>retval</code> off the stack. Makes <code>retval</code> the return value of the current function, and returns execution to the caller of the current function.

Behaviour is undefined if <code>retval</code> is not a number.

==== <code>ret.b</code>: return boolean ====

Format: <code>0x48</code>

Stack before: …, <code><retval>: boolean</code> ■

Stack after: (the stack is destroyed)

Pops <code>retval</code> off the stack. Makes <code>retval</code> the return value of the current function, and returns execution to the caller of the current function.

Behaviour is undefined if <code>retval</code> is not a boolean.

==== <code>ret.u</code>: return undefined ====

Format: <code>0x49</code>

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: (the stack is destroyed)

Makes <code>undefined</code> the return value of the current function, and returns execution to the caller of the current function.

==== <code>ret.n</code>: return null ====

Format: <code>0x4A</code>

Stack before: … ■

Stack after: (the stack is destroyed)

Makes <code>null</code> the return value of the current function, and returns execution to the caller of the current function.

==== <code>dup</code>: duplicate top of stack ====

Format: <code>0x4B</code>

Stack before: … <code><value></code> ■

Stack after: … <code><value></code>, <code><value></code> ■

Pushes a copy of the topmost value on the stack onto the stack.

A fault occurs if the stack is full, or the stack is empty.

==== <code>newenv</code>: create new environment ====

Format: <code>0x4C <size: u8></code> (2 bytes)

Does not modify the stack.

Creates a new environment whose parent is the current environment and with <code>size</code> entries. Sets the current environment to the new environment.

==== <code>popenv</code>: pop environment ====

Format: <code>0x4D</code>

Does not modify the stack.

Sets the current environment to be the parent of the current environment.

=== Primitive functions ===

'''This table is Sinter/Source's original primitive index table, copied here unchanged — it is not
what py-slang's compiler currently emits.''' py-slang's own primitive index table
(`PRIMITIVE_FUNCTIONS` in `src/engines/svml/builtins.ts`) assigns its own, much smaller set of
indices to Python builtins (e.g. `abs` &rarr; 10, `min` &rarr; 20), which mostly do '''not''' agree
with the indices below (e.g. index 10 here is `error`, not `abs`). This is harmless for py-slang's
own pure-TypeScript `SVMLInterpreter`, which is a closed system consulting the same table on both
the compile and execute side — but it means bytecode compiled by py-slang and run on a real
Sinter/Pynter binary (the native `--engine svml` pathway) will invoke the '''wrong''' primitive for
any index that collides with a different function here. Reconciling the two tables (most likely by
changing Pynter's own primitive table to match py-slang's, once Pynter is free to diverge from
Sinter) is unresolved follow-up work, not yet done as of this fork.

The functions below correspond to the functions defined in the [https://sicp.comp.nus.edu.sg/source/source_4/ Source language documentation]; they are SVML/Source's original primitive set, not PVML's.

The behaviours of <code>display</code> and <code>error</code> are implementation-defined.

* <code>0x00</code>: <code>accumulate</code>
* <code>0x01</code>: <code>append</code>
* <code>0x02</code>: <code>array_length</code>
* <code>0x03</code>: <code>build_list</code>
* <code>0x04</code>: <code>build_stream</code>
* <code>0x05</code>: <code>display</code>
* <code>0x06</code>: <code>draw_data</code>
* <code>0x07</code>: <code>enum_list</code>
* <code>0x08</code>: <code>enum_stream</code>
* <code>0x09</code>: <code>equal</code>
* <code>0x0a</code>: <code>error</code>
* <code>0x0b</code>: <code>eval_stream</code>
* <code>0x0c</code>: <code>filter</code>
* <code>0x0d</code>: <code>for_each</code>
* <code>0x0e</code>: <code>head</code>
* <code>0x0f</code>: <code>integers_from</code>
* <code>0x10</code>: <code>is_array</code>
* <code>0x11</code>: <code>is_boolean</code>
* <code>0x12</code>: <code>is_function</code>
* <code>0x13</code>: <code>is_list</code>
* <code>0x14</code>: <code>is_null</code>
* <code>0x15</code>: <code>is_number</code>
* <code>0x16</code>: <code>is_pair</code>
* <code>0x17</code>: <code>is_stream</code>
* <code>0x18</code>: <code>is_string</code>
* <code>0x19</code>: <code>is_undefined</code>
* <code>0x1a</code>: <code>length</code>
* <code>0x1b</code>: <code>list</code>
* <code>0x1c</code>: <code>list_ref</code>
* <code>0x1d</code>: <code>list_to_stream</code>
* <code>0x1e</code>: <code>list_to_string</code>
* <code>0x1f</code>: <code>map</code>
* <code>0x20</code>: <code>math_abs</code>
* <code>0x21</code>: <code>math_acos</code>
* <code>0x22</code>: <code>math_acosh</code>
* <code>0x23</code>: <code>math_asin</code>
* <code>0x24</code>: <code>math_asinh</code>
* <code>0x25</code>: <code>math_atan</code>
* <code>0x26</code>: <code>math_atan2</code>
* <code>0x27</code>: <code>math_atanh</code>
* <code>0x28</code>: <code>math_cbrt</code>
* <code>0x29</code>: <code>math_ceil</code>
* <code>0x2a</code>: <code>math_clz32</code>
* <code>0x2b</code>: <code>math_cos</code>
* <code>0x2c</code>: <code>math_cosh</code>
* <code>0x2d</code>: <code>math_exp</code>
* <code>0x2e</code>: <code>math_expm1</code>
* <code>0x2f</code>: <code>math_floor</code>
* <code>0x30</code>: <code>math_fround</code>
* <code>0x31</code>: <code>math_hypot</code>
* <code>0x32</code>: <code>math_imul</code>
* <code>0x33</code>: <code>math_log</code>
* <code>0x34</code>: <code>math_log1p</code>
* <code>0x35</code>: <code>math_log2</code>
* <code>0x36</code>: <code>math_log10</code>
* <code>0x37</code>: <code>math_max</code>
* <code>0x38</code>: <code>math_min</code>
* <code>0x39</code>: <code>math_pow</code>
* <code>0x3a</code>: <code>math_random</code>
* <code>0x3b</code>: <code>math_round</code>
* <code>0x3c</code>: <code>math_sign</code>
* <code>0x3d</code>: <code>math_sin</code>
* <code>0x3e</code>: <code>math_sinh</code>
* <code>0x3f</code>: <code>math_sqrt</code>
* <code>0x40</code>: <code>math_tan</code>
* <code>0x41</code>: <code>math_tanh</code>
* <code>0x42</code>: <code>math_trunc</code>
* <code>0x43</code>: <code>member</code>
* <code>0x44</code>: <code>pair</code>
* <code>0x45</code>: <code>parse_int</code>
* <code>0x46</code>: <code>remove</code>
* <code>0x47</code>: <code>remove_all</code>
* <code>0x48</code>: <code>reverse</code>
* <code>0x49</code>: <code>runtime</code>
* <code>0x4a</code>: <code>set_head</code>
* <code>0x4b</code>: <code>set_tail</code>
* <code>0x4c</code>: <code>stream</code>
* <code>0x4d</code>: <code>stream_append</code>
* <code>0x4e</code>: <code>stream_filter</code>
* <code>0x4f</code>: <code>stream_for_each</code>
* <code>0x50</code>: <code>stream_length</code>
* <code>0x51</code>: <code>stream_map</code>
* <code>0x52</code>: <code>stream_member</code>
* <code>0x53</code>: <code>stream_ref</code>
* <code>0x54</code>: <code>stream_remove</code>
* <code>0x55</code>: <code>stream_remove_all</code>
* <code>0x56</code>: <code>stream_reverse</code>
* <code>0x57</code>: <code>stream_tail</code>
* <code>0x58</code>: <code>stream_to_list</code>
* <code>0x59</code>: <code>tail</code>
* <code>0x5a</code>: <code>stringify</code>
* <code>0x5b</code>: <code>prompt</code>

=== Machine-parseable instruction set ===

A JSON dump of the instruction set follows.

<pre>[
  [0, "nop", "no-op"],
  [1, "ldc.i", "load constant integer"],
  [2, "lgc.i", "load boxed constant integer"],
  [3, "ldc.f32", "load constant number (single-precision)"],
  [4, "lgc.f32", "load boxed constant number (single-precision)"],
  [5, "ldc.f64", "load constant number (double-precision)"],
  [6, "lgc.f64", "load boxed constant number (double-precision)"],
  [7, "ldc.b.0", "load constant false"],
  [8, "ldc.b.1", "load constant true"],
  [9, "lgc.b.0", "load boxed constant false"],
  [10, "lgc.b.1", "load boxed constant true"],
  [11, "lgc.u", "load boxed constant undefined"],
  [12, "lgc.n", "load boxed constant null"],
  [13, "lgc.s", "load constant string"],
  [14, "pop.g", "pop boxed value from stack"],
  [15, "pop.b", "pop boolean from stack"],
  [16, "pop.f", "pop number from stack"],
  [17, "add.g", "add boxed values"],
  [18, "add.f", "add numbers"],
  [19, "sub.g", "subtract boxed values"],
  [20, "sub.f", "subtract numbers"],
  [21, "mul.g", "multiply boxed values"],
  [22, "mul.f", "multiply numbers"],
  [23, "div.g", "divide boxed values"],
  [24, "div.f", "divide numbers"],
  [25, "mod.g", "modulo boxed values"],
  [26, "mod.f", "modulo numbers"],
  [27, "not.g", "negate boxed value"],
  [28, "not.b", "negate boolean"],
  [29, "lt.g", "less than, boxed operands"],
  [30, "lt.f", "less than, number operands"],
  [31, "gt.g", "greater than, boxed operands"],
  [32, "gt.f", "greater than, number operands"],
  [33, "le.g", "less than or equal to, boxed operands"],
  [34, "le.f", "less than or equal to, number operands"],
  [35, "ge.g", "greater than or equal to, boxed operands"],
  [36, "ge.f", "greater than or equal to, number operands"],
  [37, "eq.g", "equal, boxed operands"],
  [38, "eq.f", "equal, number operands"],
  [39, "eq.b", "equal, boolean operands"],
  [40, "new.c", "create function"],
  [41, "new.a", "create array"],
  [42, "ldl.g", "load boxed value from current environment"],
  [43, "ldl.f", "load number from current environment"],
  [44, "ldl.b", "load boolean from current environment"],
  [45, "stl.g", "store boxed value into current environment"],
  [46, "stl.b", "store boolean into current environment"],
  [47, "stl.f", "store number into current environment"],
  [48, "ldp.g", "load boxed value from (parent) environment"],
  [49, "ldp.f", "load number from (parent) environment"],
  [50, "ldp.b", "load boolean from (parent) environment"],
  [51, "stp.g", "store boxed value into (parent) environment"],
  [52, "stp.b", "store boolean into (parent) environment"],
  [53, "stp.f", "store number into (parent) environment"],
  [54, "lda.g", "load boxed value from array"],
  [55, "lda.b", "load boolean from array"],
  [56, "lda.f", "load number from array"],
  [57, "sta.g", "store boxed value into array"],
  [58, "sta.b", "store boolean into array"],
  [59, "sta.f", "store number into array"],
  [60, "br.t", "branch if true"],
  [61, "br.f", "branch if false"],
  [62, "br", "branch"],
  [63, "jmp", "jump"],
  [64, "call", "call function"],
  [65, "call.t", "tail call function"],
  [66, "call.p", "call primitive function"],
  [67, "call.t.p", "tail call primitive function"],
  [68, "call.v", "call VM-internal function/native function"],
  [69, "call.t.v", "tail call VM-internal function/native function"],
  [70, "ret.g", "return boxed value"],
  [71, "ret.f", "return number"],
  [72, "ret.b", "return boolean"],
  [73, "ret.u", "return undefined"],
  [74, "ret.n", "return null"],
  [75, "dup", "duplicate top of stack"],
  [76, "newenv", "create new environment"],
  [77, "popenv", "pop environment"],
  [78, "new.c.p", "create primitive function object"],
  [79, "new.c.v", "create VM-internal function object"],
  [80, "neg.g", "negate boxed value"],
  [81, "neg.f", "negate number"],
  [82, "neq.g", "not equal, boxed operands"],
  [83, "neq.f", "not equal, number operands"],
  [84, "neq.b", "not equal, boolean operands"]
]</pre>
